title: Shallow Water
description:
**Shallow Water Equations.** Depth-averaged free-surface flow with bed topography and optional Manning friction (`n_m`); the level-0 base model.

Default case: 1-D wall-bounded dam break (h = 2 | 1 at x = 5), domain 0..10 with 50 cells, marched by **Hyperbolic (NumPy)**.
template:
import numpy as np
from zoomy_core.model.models import SWE
import zoomy_core.model.boundary_conditions as BC
import zoomy_core.model.initial_conditions as IC
from zoomy_core.systemmodel import SystemModel

# Wall-bounded 1-D dam break: closed box, h = 2 (left) | 1 (right) at x = 5.
# Pair with "Create 1D" (0..10, 50 cells) + "Hyperbolic (NumPy)".
# The wall is DEFINED IN THE DERIVATION (register_free_slip_wall): the ghost
# mirrors the momentum VECTOR about the face -- every moment / layer / mode
# reflected direction-wise -- so ONE definition serves every model, mesh and
# orientation, with no state-layout guessing.  b/h extrapolate.
# Swap BC.FromModel <-> BC.Extrapolation / BC.InflowOutflow / BC.Periodic per tag.
model = SystemModel.from_model(SWE(
    dimension=1,
    parameters={"g": 9.81, "n_m": 0.0},
    boundary_conditions=BC.BoundaryConditions(
        [BC.FromModel(tag="left", definition="wall"),
         BC.FromModel(tag="right", definition="wall")]),
    initial_conditions=IC.RP(
        high=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 2.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        low=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 1.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        jump_position_x=5.0)))

title: Shallow Moments
description:
**Shallow Moment Equations (SME).** Depth-averaged flow resolving a polynomial vertical velocity profile through `level` moments (Kowalski & Torrilhon); `level = 0` recovers shallow water. `dimension` counts the vertical too, so `dimension = 2` is a 1-D horizontal model.

Default case: 1-D wall-bounded dam break (h = 2 | 1 at x = 5), domain 0..10 with 50 cells, marched by **Hyperbolic (NumPy)**.
template:
import numpy as np
from zoomy_core.model.models import SME, Newtonian, NavierSlip, StressFree
import zoomy_core.model.boundary_conditions as BC
import zoomy_core.model.initial_conditions as IC
from zoomy_core.systemmodel import SystemModel

# Wall-bounded 1-D dam break: closed box, h = 2 (left) | 1 (right) at x = 5.
# dimension=2 is the TOTAL dimension (t, x, z) -> ONE horizontal direction.
# The wall is DEFINED IN THE DERIVATION (register_free_slip_wall): the ghost
# mirrors the momentum VECTOR about the face -- every moment / layer / mode
# reflected direction-wise -- so ONE definition serves every model, mesh and
# orientation, with no state-layout guessing.  b/h extrapolate.
# Swap BC.FromModel <-> BC.Extrapolation / BC.InflowOutflow / BC.Periodic per tag.
# Pair with "Create 1D" (0..10, 50 cells) + "Hyperbolic (NumPy)".
model = SystemModel.from_model(SME(
    level=2,
    dimension=2,
    closures=[Newtonian(), NavierSlip(), StressFree()],
    boundary_conditions=BC.BoundaryConditions(
        [BC.FromModel(tag="left", definition="wall"),
         BC.FromModel(tag="right", definition="wall")]),
    initial_conditions=IC.RP(
        high=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 2.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        low=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 1.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        jump_position_x=5.0)))

title: Multi-layer Shallow Moments
description:
**Multi-layer Shallow Moments.** `n_layers` surface-following layers, each carrying `level + 1` velocity moments, coupled by an interface mass-flux closure (`UpwindInterface`).

Default case: 1-D wall-bounded dam break (h = 2 | 1 at x = 5), domain 0..10 with 50 cells, marched by **Hyperbolic (NumPy)**.

⚠ Needs a `zoomy-core` newer than the PyPI 0.2.5 wheel Pyodide installs: the layered wall needs the derived momentum groups, so this card is the one that does not build in the browser yet.
template:
import numpy as np
from zoomy_core.model.models import (MLSME, Newtonian, NavierSlip, StressFree,
                                     UpwindInterface)
import zoomy_core.model.boundary_conditions as BC
import zoomy_core.model.initial_conditions as IC
from zoomy_core.systemmodel import SystemModel

# Wall-bounded 1-D dam break: closed box, h = 2 (left) | 1 (right) at x = 5.
# dimension=2 is the TOTAL dimension (t, x, z) -> ONE horizontal direction.
# The wall is DEFINED IN THE DERIVATION (register_free_slip_wall): the ghost
# mirrors the momentum VECTOR about the face -- every moment / layer / mode
# reflected direction-wise -- so ONE definition serves every model, mesh and
# orientation, with no state-layout guessing.  b/h extrapolate.
# Swap BC.FromModel <-> BC.Extrapolation / BC.InflowOutflow / BC.Periodic per tag.
model = SystemModel.from_model(MLSME(
    n_layers=2,
    level=1,
    dimension=2,
    closures=[Newtonian(), NavierSlip(), StressFree(), UpwindInterface()],
    boundary_conditions=BC.BoundaryConditions(
        [BC.FromModel(tag="left", definition="wall"),
         BC.FromModel(tag="right", definition="wall")]),
    initial_conditions=IC.RP(
        high=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 2.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        low=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 1.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        jump_position_x=5.0)))

title: VAM
description:
**Vertically-Averaged Moment model (VAM).** Non-hydrostatic moment closure (`level` = truncation order) for dispersive shallow flow; u, w and the non-hydrostatic pressure share the basis.

Default case: 1-D wall-bounded dam break (h = 2 | 1 at x = 5), domain 0..10 with 50 cells. **Needs the Split (NumPy) solver** (Chorin projection) — the plain hyperbolic march leaves the non-hydrostatic pressure at zero. The card binds `split` for it.
template:
import numpy as np
from zoomy_core.model.models import VAM, Newtonian, NavierSlip, StressFree
import zoomy_core.model.boundary_conditions as BC
import zoomy_core.model.initial_conditions as IC
from zoomy_core.systemmodel import SystemModel

# Wall-bounded 1-D dam break: closed box, h = 2 (left) | 1 (right) at x = 5.
# VAM is NON-HYDROSTATIC: it must be marched with the Chorin projection, so
# this card also binds `split` (predictor / pressure / corrector stages) for
# the "Split / Chorin (NumPy)" solver card.  The plain "Hyperbolic (NumPy)"
# card would march it with P frozen at 0, i.e. silently hydrostatic.
vam = VAM(
    level=1,
    dimension=2,
    closures=[Newtonian(), NavierSlip(), StressFree()],
    boundary_conditions=BC.BoundaryConditions(
        [BC.FromModel(tag="left", definition="wall"),
         BC.FromModel(tag="right", definition="wall")]),
    initial_conditions=IC.RP(
        high=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 2.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        low=lambda n: np.array([0.0, 1.0] + [0.0] * (n - 2)),
        jump_position_x=5.0))

model = SystemModel.from_model(vam)
split = vam.chorin_split(system_model=model)
